programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a wine launch turns deadly, Barnaby tries to find out who is targeting the Midsomer Vinae Winery and what it has to do with the death of a child in a hit-and-run accident.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death exposing a sinister web of secrets and lies in the village of Little Malton.

programme.title: Magnum P.I.
programme.desc: Higgins is in a complicated situation when 12-year-old Ella asks for help cracking a triple homicide that involves her mother; Magnum bonds with a terminally ill veteran while volunteering with the No Veteran Dies Alone Program.

programme.title: Magnum P.I.
programme.desc: Magnum goes to extreme lengths to uncover the truth about a crime that may have involved his dear deceased friend.

programme.title: Magnum P.I.
programme.desc: A man wakes up on a beach covered in blood, so he hires Magnum and Higgins to investigate a crime he doesn't remember; T.C. is taken aback when Cade's biological grandmother makes a surprise visit; Rick's feelings for Suzy grow stronger.

programme.title: Magnum P.I.
programme.desc: Magnum and Higgins are sent on a deadly mission inside a high security correctional center when a ruthless criminal abducts Katsumoto and his ex-wife; T.C. rushes to help Rick when Suzy experiences serious pregnancy complications.

programme.title: Magnum P.I.
programme.desc: Magnum and Higgins weigh the risks of pursuing a relationship; Rick juggles being a new father and running La Mariana; Katsumoto tries to figure out his next move.

programme.title: Magnum
programme.desc: Magnum and Higgins go undercover as lifeguards to solve a murder; Jin Jeong wins a storage unit auction and enlists the help of Rick and TC upon finding a stolen military medal.

programme.title: Killer Whale
programme.desc: Best friends Maddie and Trish find themselves trapped in a remote lagoon with a dangerous killer whale.

programme.title: Burning Bright
programme.desc: Two siblings are trapped in a house during a hurricane and must fight for their lives against a tiger.

programme.title: Rogue
programme.desc: A rugged American travel journalist on assignment in the Australian outback encounters a man-eating crocodile.

programme.title: Primal
programme.desc: On a ship, an arrested assassin breaks free and releases a 400 lbs. white jaguar.

programme.title: Bait 3D
programme.desc: A freak tsunami traps a group of people inside a supermarket, and soon they find they are not alone.

programme.title: Just Getting Started
programme.desc: Duke Diver is living the high life as the freewheeling manager of a luxurious resort in Palm Springs, Calif. He soon faces competition from Leo, a former military man who likes the same woman that Duke is interested in. When Diver's past suddenly catches up with him, he must put aside his differences and reluctantly team up with Leo to stop whoever is trying to kill him.

programme.title: The Code
programme.desc: A veteran thief recruits a younger crook to help him pull off one final job in order to repay his debt to the Russian mob.

programme.title: Kill Switch
programme.desc: A psychopathic killer is ravaging Memphis, Tennesse by murdering young women and Detective Jacob Stillwell is on the case. Known as the resident tough cop due to his aggressive tactics, he gets knocked sideways when his girlfriend becomes one of the murder victims. With his partner, Storm Anderson, in tow, Jacob casts the rules aside and infiltrates the seedy criminal underworld of the city looking to avenge his lover's death and put an end to the killings.

programme.title: Blitz
programme.desc: Jason Statham stars as a tough cop who's dispatched to take down a serial killer who has been targeting police officers. Also featuring Paddy Considine and Aidan Gillen.

programme.title: Brotherhood Of The Wolf
programme.desc: In 18th-century France, the Chevalier de Fronsac and his Native American friend Mani are sent to the Gevaudan province.

programme.title: The Pledge
programme.desc: A retiring police detective pledges to catch the killer of a young child.

programme.title: Two For the Money
programme.desc: Brandon Lang loves football: an injury keeps him from the pros.

programme.title: Blue Thunder
programme.desc: A tough but straight-arrow cop battles a group of military and government elitists who have developed an extraordinary helicopter into the ultimate weapon. Edited for TV.

programme.title: Conspiracy
programme.desc: A seasoned senior enlisted special operations United States Marine is wounded during combat operations in Iraq. He retires from the Marine Corps and visits a friend in the south west. There he discovers his friend has disappeared and no one will even admit to knowing of him.

programme.title: Mortal Thoughts
programme.desc: Two New Jersey hairdressers try to cover up a murder without destroying their friendship.

programme.title: The Da Vinci Code
programme.desc: A murder in the Louvre and clues in Da Vinci paintings lead to the discovery of a religious mystery protected by a secret society for two thousand years -- which could shake the foundations of Christianity.

programme.title: Theodora Goes Wild
programme.desc: Riotous comedy and zany romance fuel this story of a small-town girl (Irene Dunne) who writes a racy best-selling novel and falls in love with the New York sophisticate (Melvyn Douglas) who illustrates it. A fine supporting cast keeps this film rolling along splendidly.

programme.title: Hook, Line And Sinker
programme.desc: Thinking he's dying, a poor schnook goes on a $100,000 spending spree only to find out he's as healthy as a horse.

programme.title: Oliver!
programme.desc: Charles Dickens classic tale of a waif in 19th Century England comes alive brilliantly in this Oscar-winning musical. Fleeing a life of workhouse servitude, Oliver (Mark Lester) arrives in London to seek his fortune. During his adventures, Oliver meets a gallery of unforgettable Dickens low-lifes.

programme.title: A Man For All Seasons
programme.desc: Dramatic depiction of the conflict between Henry VIII and Sir Thomas More. Winner of six Oscars, including Best Picture (1966), best director (Fred Zinnemann), and best actor (Paul Scofield).

programme.title: Stay Cool
programme.desc: An author who returns to his hometown to deliver a commencement address to a class of graduating high school students has to deal with his feelings for an old flame as well as the advances of a student who has the hots for him.

programme.title: Freejack
programme.desc: Bounty hunters from the future transport a doomed race car driver to New York City in 2009, where his mind will be replaced with that of a dead billionaire.

programme.title: The Kid
programme.desc: The Tramp cares for an abandoned child, but events put that relationship in jeopardy.

programme.title: The Getaway
programme.desc: An ex-con and his devoted wife must flee from danger when a heist doesn't go as planned.

programme.title: A Fantastic Fear Of Everything
programme.desc: A crime novelist whose research on Victorian serial killers has turned him into a paranoid wreck must confront his worst fears when a film executive takes a sudden interest in his movie script.

programme.title: Blood Brother
programme.desc: Jake goes to prison to protect his friends, and one becomes a cop. When Jake is released, he seeks revenge.

programme.title: Acts of Violence
programme.desc: When his fiancee is kidnapped by traffickers, Roman and his ex-military brothers set out to track her down.

programme.title: Set Up
programme.desc: After a heist, a man is betrayed and vows to get revenge, joining forces with a ruthless crime lord.

programme.title: Replicant
programme.desc: In order to destroy the future's most ruthless killer, they had to create him.

programme.title: Ticker
programme.desc: After a detective's partner is murdered by Northern Irish terrorists, the officer joins the bomb squad.

programme.title: Salvation
programme.desc: A foster child bonds with a snake-handling Pentecostal community but uncovers sinister truths. She must choose between reporting pastor's crimes or preserving her newfound support system, risking personal safety for a sense of belonging.

programme.title: A Witness to Murder
programme.desc: Sanitation worker MaryBeth is in the wrong place at the wrong time when the mayor's wife is murdered. She refuses to talk to the sheriff she dislikes, but the killers know she was there. Now they're coming for her to make sure she stays silent.

programme.title: Prey of Wrath
programme.desc: An elite special forces operative is sent by Russian Intelligence to hunt down former teammates who have hijacked nuclear warheads.

programme.title: Joe Baby
programme.desc: A money collector for a private investigator is hired to track down 10 million dollars stolen in a dodgy real estate deal, opening a complex can of worms.

programme.title: Lone Star Shark
programme.desc: A group of virtual hunters unleash a deadly shark in a Texas lake, causing a young couple to fight for their lives during a peaceful fishing trip in a secluded cove.

programme.title: A Janitor
programme.desc: Fukumi is a high school janitor whose father was a gangster. He works at the school as a janitor instead of belonging to the gang so he can watch out for Yui, the daughter of his deceased father's brother Majima Yoshiki.

programme.title: The Foggy Mountain
programme.desc: A fearless martial-arts master seeks revenge for the murder of his wife.

programme.title: The Golden Cane Warrior
programme.desc: When an aging martial arts master chooses two of her meeker students to be her successors, two older students steal her mystical cane and pursue their rivals.

programme.title: The Bodyguard
programme.desc: A retired bodyguard in the early stages of dementia befriends a young girl. When her father's criminal connections place her in danger, the bodyguard calls upon forgotten skills to keep her safe.

programme.title: Eye for an Eye
programme.desc: A highly trained and exceptionally deadly swordsman looks for revenge after being caught in the middle of a power struggle that results in the assassination of an innocent woman's family.

programme.title: I Was Octomom: The Natalie Suleman Story
programme.desc: Showcasing how "Octomom" overcame overwhelming odds to raise her children.

programme.title: I Was Honey Boo Boo
programme.desc: Alana Thompson, famously known as "Honey Boo Boo," narrates this Lifetime biopic.

programme.title: Cellmate Secrets
programme.desc: Mitchell's former manager and prison security supervisor are speaking out.

programme.title: I Was Lorena Bobbitt
programme.desc: 26 years since Lorena Bobbitt's notorious abuse case.

programme.title: I Was Octomom: The Natalie Suleman Story
programme.desc: Showcasing how "Octomom" overcame overwhelming odds to raise her children.

programme.title: Fatal Performance
programme.desc: A struggling actress is hired to impersonate a woman for a business meeting, but soon finds her life in danger.

programme.title: Wild Faith
programme.desc: A Civil War vet makes peace with his losses and starts a new life with the world resisting change around him.

programme.title: Storm Cell
programme.desc: Storm chaser April, whose parents died in a twister during her childhood, must warn her brother and his unsespecting town of a massive storm heading their way before he meets the same fate.

programme.title: Stolen Daughter
programme.desc: A female police detective's teenage daughter is kidnapped by an emotionally disturbed parolee.

programme.title: Stressed To Death
programme.desc: At a robbery at a mini mart an EMT is faced with two victims; a pregnant lady and a man shot but still alive.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: In three defining chapters, Chiron, a young Black man growing up in Miami, is guided to manhood by the kindness, support, and love of his community.

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: Former Marine John "Falcon" Chapman is a dark anti-hero driven by guilt and suffering from PTSD, who will destroy himself unless given something else to destroy - a useful weapon-of-last-resort for the U.S. State Department.

programme.title: On Angel's Wings
programme.desc: When an angel grants a struggling water girl (Taylor Faye Ruffin) three wishes, even her mom (Robin Givens) or coach (Reginald VelJohnson) can't figure out how she became a soccer superstar.

programme.title: Hard Lesson
programme.desc: A Jewish parole officer, Jeff Solomon White, struggles in his new role as a reading teacher for illiterate teens at a New York high school. Raised by African American parents, White sees the situation differently and is motivated to fight for his students, who face an almost certain life of crime and prison.

programme.title: Just Getting Started
programme.desc: Duke Diver is living the high life as the freewheeling manager of a luxurious resort in Palm Springs, Calif. He soon faces competition from Leo, a former military man who likes the same woman that Duke is interested in. When Diver's past suddenly catches up with him, he must put aside his differences and reluctantly team up with Leo to stop whoever is trying to kill him.

programme.title: Her Best Revenge
programme.desc: After a tragic death, five fraternity bother reunite at a vacation home, while the shadow of a death from their past lingers over them. A mysterious figure begins to send them threatening messages. Their secrets and loyalties are put to the test and they must uncover the truth about what happened that night five years ago.

programme.title: Love Kills
programme.desc: In the heart of Atlanta's unpredictable dating scene, Darron is a hopeless romantic with the worst luck imaginable. Every time he believes he's found "the one," he ends up caught in another tangled web of passion, betrayal, and danger. When Darron finally meets someone who seems genuine, his past mistakes come back with a vengeance. One of his exes' jealous boyfriends discovers their hidden affair, setting off a chain of events that spirals out of control.

programme.title: Taking What's Mine
programme.desc: Jordan Hayes, a decorated Army veteran turned rising political icon is thrown into a harrowing disaster when her journalist husband, Ethan, is captured by a rising terrorist leader, Khalid Rami. Rami will only release Ethan if Jordan helps overturn a new counter terrorism bill. Refusing to fight the red tape and bow to pressure, Jordan takes matters into her own hands and sets out to rescue him herself.

programme.title: Transcend
programme.desc: Maranda spent her life looking for a protector, only to find herself lost in a cycle of toxic love and betrayal. From the wreckage of abandonment to a life-altering crossroads, her survival isn't just about escaping the past, it’s about surviving herself.

programme.title: ToxiCity
programme.desc: Corey Spencer, the innovator behind the successful ATLUV dating app, navigates a complex personal life. Despite his app's mission, he struggles with love, further complicated by a child support order. A night out leads him to Sierra, an alluring actress who quickly moves in. Their romance sours as Sierra's past as a dancer and her aggressive jealousy come to light.

programme.title: Stick Em Game
programme.desc: Struggling to launch her social media career, Elise decides to switch to viral pranks. When one prank on an old acquaintance goes terribly wrong, a local gang leader is out for revenge. With her best friend’s help, Elise must fight for her life to survive.

programme.title: Blood Lake
programme.desc: Thousands of carnivorous fish attack citizens of a sleepy lake town, leaving them scrambling to stay alive.

programme.title: Mega Shark VS Kolossus
programme.desc: Russia accidentally reawakens a Cold War giant robot doomsday device the same time a new Mega Shark appears.

programme.title: Mega Shark vs Mecha Shark
programme.desc: When Mega Shark returns, the government unleashes the top-secret aquatic robot to defeat the monster.

programme.title: Shark Warning!
programme.desc: A man returns home years after his brother was killed by a shark, but the shark is back. He must get revenge.

programme.title: Sharknado
programme.desc: When a hurricane swamps Los Angeles, thousands of sharks terrorize the waterlogged populace.

programme.title: Itsy Bitsy
programme.desc: A story tangled up in a web of family drama, personal demons, and a horrifying monster.Based on the centuries-old poem, a family moves into a secluded mansion where they soon find themselves being targeted by an ancient entity taking the form of a giant spider.Kara, a private nurse and single mother, moves from the big city to the quiet countryside with her two children, 13-year-old Jesse and 8-year-old Cambria, to live with and take care of an elderly man with multiple sclerosis. Things seem normal enough until Jesse discovers a mysterious old relic in the old man's house, leading to unexpected and horrifying things coming to life.

programme.title: Creeping Death
programme.desc: Halloween was always Tims favorite holidayuntil his mother was diagnosed with brain cancer. Now, all he wants is a quiet night in. That is quickly disrupted when his friends pay him an unwanted visit, after stealing a mysterious sack from the doorstep of a local crazy. This sacrilegious act unleashes the Aos Si, a Celtic spirit that walks the Earth on Halloween. They must band together and survive the night or make the ultimate offeringtheir lives--this Halloween, all who trick are treats.

programme.title: The Toxic Avenger Part 2
programme.desc: Toxie's mean, green, and back on the screen! Melvin Junko was a nerdy 98lb weakling until he fell into a vat of toxic waste, turning him into the first ever superhuman superhero from New Jersey. This time the hideously deformed creature of superhuman size and strength takes on Tokyo. The Toxic Avenger Part II is a spectacular sequel with even more ingenious special effects, even more bizarre action, and even more unbelievably famous fight scenes. He's come a long way from Tromaville, baby!

programme.title: Beast from Haunted Cave
programme.desc: A giant spider stalks a band of thieves in this schlocky thriller from Gene Corman Productions. The story follows gangster Alexander Ward and his crew as they enlist the help of a local ski-instructor in the heist of several gold bars. But when they use explosives to create a distraction, they disturb the lair of a massive spider! Monte Hellman, the man behind such cult favorites as Two-Lane Blacktop, made his directorial debut with Beast From Haunted Cave. Trivia note: This movie debuted on a double bill with The Wasp Woman.

programme.title: Humanoids From The Deep (Edited Version)
programme.desc: A race of scientifically created half-human, half-salmon monstrosities lay siege to a Pacific Northwest fishing village. In horrific fashion, the mutated creatures kill the men and mate with the women in order to reproduce. Can the Humanoids From the Deep be stopped before they destroy mankind as we know it?Soundtrack scored by Oscar-winning composer James Horner (Avatar, Titanic, Braveheart). Creature design from Oscar-winning make-up artist Rob Bottin (Total Recall, The Thing, Robocop, Se7en).

programme.title: Horror Noire
programme.desc: Horror Noire

programme.title: Ghost Stories
programme.desc: A man looking to debunk a series of paranormal events falls into a world of terror. Martin Freeman stars in a tale that will haunt your dreams.

programme.title: Babadook, The
programme.desc: In this terrifying thriller, a mother must protect her son from an evil, supernatural entity that has escaped the pages of a children's book and is lurking in their home.

programme.title: Come True
programme.desc: Looking for an escape from her recurring nightmares, 18-year-old Sarah submits to a university sleep study, but soon realizes she's become the conduit to a frightening new discovery.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Spring Tonic" - Miss Hathaway's brilliant-but-frumpy bank protegee Gloria is really a sultry gold-digger with eyes for Jed's $34 million, and Jed's just had his annual dose of Granny's spring tonic.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Cuts the Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale continues to be forced into doing "practices from the past" by Mrs. Smith-Standish, who discovers that Jed's family is possibly the first to come the country. If so they'd be famous worldwide, but does Jed want that?

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale wants to get rid of the Clampetts before the arrival of Mrs. Smith-Standish, the head of a 'first family' historical society. But Mrs. Smith-Standish goes to the Clampetts' first and becomes enchanted with their antiques and way of life, even to roping the horrified Mrs. Drysdale into helping do the chores the old-fashioned way.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Becomes a Banker" - Drysdale hires Jed to be a banker when he needs a crack shot to help beat a rival banker in a skeet shooting competition, but his partner needs to be an employee.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Buys the Freeway" - Henry Jones shows up at the Clampett's, pretends to be an old friend and tries to sell the family the Hollywood Bowl, Griffith Park, and the freeway.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Duke Steals a Wife" - Jed and Duke his dog are both down without a woman in their lives, until they see and pursue a French woman and her poodle that she brought to breed with Mrs. Drysdales dog. Meanwhile Mrs. Drysdale has arranged a "marriage" between the dogs, complete with a decorated bedroom.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Plays Solomon" - Granny can't stand Pearl's yodeling so she reports her to the police. When one of the officers takes Pearl out, Granny thinks she got Pearl in trouble and becomes sad and contrite. Meanwhile, the police find her still, and new trouble results.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Throws a Wingding" - Flatt and Scruggs and their wives come to visit their old sweetheart, but Pearl thinks they're there to propose. Jed throws a wingding for them and the duo play music for the Clampetts.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly's Animals" - When Pearl starts selling music lessons, Mrs. Drysdale complains about the noise to the police. Mrs. Drysdale also calls the dog catcher on Duke.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Saves Drysdale's Marriage" - Mrs Drysdale leaves for a health farm saying Mr. Drysdale is "going to have a new wife," while Mr. Drysdale tells Cousin Pearl he'd like her to keep his house. However, the Clampett's think he's wanting to marry Pearl, so Jed comes up with a plan to "save" the Drysdale's marriage.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed's Dilemma" - Grannie and Cousin Pearl are at each other's throats over who's going to take care of cooking and the house, so Jed has to find ways to keep them apart.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

With a cast that inclues Andy Devine as Sheriff Cookie Bullfincer, director William Witney gives us an enjoyable drama from the golden era of Republic Pictures!

programme.title: Home in Oklahoma

programme.title: Way of the West
programme.desc: Government agent Gordon (Hal Taliaferro) goes undercover to investigate an ongoing war between cattlemen and sheepmen. But he soon finds himself framed for murder! This low budget western from the short-lived Empire Pictures is from the golden era of shoot em ups, when audiences knew who the bad guys were, and couldnt wait for the good guys to serve up some just punishment!

programme.title: Mohawk
programme.desc: Kurt Neumann (The Fly, Rocketship X-M) directed this bawdy tale of a love that was louder than war drums! With a cast that includes Scott Brady, Rita Gam, and Neville Brand, the story takes place on a remote army post where an artist romances a trio of women, just as a vengeful settler turns the army and the local Iroquois tribe against each other. Film buffs might like to know that the cinematography for this feature is by four-time Oscar nominee Karl Struss, who won an Academy Award in 1929 for his work on Sunrise.

programme.title: McLintock
programme.desc: He likes his whiskey hard... His women soft... And his west all to himself! Inspired by Shakespeares The Taming of the Shrew (1594), John Wayne (The Searchers, Stagecoach), Maureen OHara (Miracle on 34th Street, The Parent Trap), and Patrick Wayne (The Searchers, The Alamo) star in this western comedy directed by Andrew V. McLaglen (Gunsmoke, The Wild Geese). George Washington McLintock (John Wayne) is the wealthiest man in his territory who utilizes his power and influence to maintain order between farmers, ranchers, Indians, corrupt government officials, and his own family! McLintock is McNificent!

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The Chewed Up crew sets up poolside at the W Hotel South Beach for a breezy St. Patrick's Day episode that swaps corned beef for Cuban grilled shrimp. Michael Symon, whose Cleveland neighborhood is deeply Irish, admits he's been celebrating St. Patrick's Day with his best friend Jimmy for decades, and this year Jimmy joins them in Miami. Michael's Grilled Shrimp Mojo is a tribute to the Cuban flavors of Miami: shrimp marinated in lime zest, lime juice, chili flakes, and olive oil, then grilled and finished with a vibrant mojo sauce blended from sour orange, onion, a whole head of garlic, cilantro, parsley, and oregano. The key tip: cook shrimp until they look like commas, not O'sóthe moment they curl completely is the moment they're overdone. Clinton reflects on his seven years living in Miami and the city's culinary depth, from Cuban staples to modern restaurants like Yardbird. Carla raises her fear of sharks; Clinton counters with his inexplicable terror of birds getting caught in his hair. Michael confesses he bought a bathing suit specifically to go to the beach, put it on, saw himself in the mirror, and decided to spend the afternoon in bed instead.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Just in time for National Ravioli Day, acclaimed chef and Chopped judge Scott Conant joins the trio at the W South Beach to make his delicate ricotta gnocchiótechnically gnudióalongside his signature Martone Street jarred tomato sauce. Michael has been close friends with Scott for 15 years, and Clinton admits he was genuinely nervous before meeting the notoriously stern Chopped judge, quickly discovering he's warm and funny in person. Scott explains that Martone Street is named for his grandparents' home in Waterbury, Connecticut, where Sunday dinners taught him that conversation over food is one of life's greatest gifts. The gnocchi dough combines fresh ricotta, basil, breadcrumbs, flour, egg, and Parmesan, shaped into small dumplings and cooked until they float. Scott builds a stabilized butter sauce using pasta water and cornstarch as an emulsifieróa trick that keeps it smooth for hours. Finished with Martone Street sauce and broccoli rabe, the result is stunning in its simplicity. His philosophy runs throughout: spaghetti aglio e olio, done correctly, is one of the most complex and satisfying dishes imaginable. Scott becomes emotional talking about watching his 16-year-old daughter drive for the first time and the joy of steak cook-offs with his girls at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Food Network Star winner and Southern food authority Damaris Phillips brings a taste of Louisville poolside at the W South Beach, demonstrating three Southern tea sandwich classics that are anything but fussy. Carla Hall has been singing Damaris's praises for yearsómutual friend Jackie Guido insisted they were destined to be peas and carrotsóand the episode proves her right, the two radiating warmth and joy throughout. Damaris makes Pimento Cheese; Benedictine, a Louisville specialty of grated cucumber with dill and cream cheese (the key being squeezing out all the cucumber water first); and Ham Salad, essentially chicken salad's porky cousin, versatile as either sweet or savory. Everything is layered on Japanese milk bread or thin white bread, crusts removed, and assembled into dainty finger sandwiches and triple-decker stacks. Michael immediately tries to build an enormous open-face sandwich rather than the delicate bites Damaris intended, sparking a lively debate about proportion and restraint. Carla shares that she recently spent a cozy Super Sunday with Damaris in Louisvilleóa beloved tradition of soups, board games, and community. The episode also features a spirited group effort to talk Damaris out of her plan to get a leopard-print face tattoo, with limited success. The episode ends with genuine laughter, warm hugs, and unanimous agreement: the sandwiches are delicious.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Two-time Tournament of Champions winner and Chopped veteran Maneet Chauhan arrives at the W South Beach as a true Queen of Flavor, demonstrating her Indian Spiced Ceviche, a dish that applies Indian culinary logic to coastal Latin technique. Fresh fish is cured in lime juice in the traditional ceviche manner, then transformed with coconut milk to soften the heat and balance the citrus, and finished with a tarka: the Indian technique of blooming whole spices in searing hot coconut oil and pouring it directly over the dish at the last second. The result is layered, bright, and unexpected. Maneet explains that she grew up in a small town in India and learned to cook by exploring her neighbors' kitchens, each representing a different regional tradition. She recounts a pivotal story from Peru, where she made potato pancakes alongside a local woman without sharing a single word of common languageóconnected entirely through food and gesture. That philosophy runs through her Nashville restaurant, which introduces diners to bold Indian flavors through fusion dishes like hot chicken korma and chicken tikka salad poutine. She draws a parallel between the tarka and the French beurre blancóboth finishing techniques that transform a dish in the final seconds of cooking. Michael, Clinton, and Carla taste the ceviche with genuine surprise and awe.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: As the Chewed Up crew wraps up their week at the W South Beach, Miami-born chef Mika Leon brings the most authentically local dish of the entire trip: Pan Con Lechon, the slow-roasted Cuban pork sandwich that anchors Noche Buena, Cuban Christmas Eve. Mika woke up at 4 AM to start the pork shoulderómarinated overnight in a mojo sauce built on sour orange juice, lime juice, orange juice, and 50 cloves of garlic from her grandfather's recipe. After six hours at 350 degrees, the pork is shredded and crisped with additional mojo in a hot pan, then piled high on a special Cuban rollósweeter and fattier than regular bread, made with lard throughout. Mika shares her journey from dabbling in TV production (she quit after a week) to opening a food truck selling $5 Pan Con Lechons at 3 AM to Miami club-goers, which grew into a full restaurant and now a cookbook. Carla notes that the best cooks often learn from family before any formal training, and this dish proves it. The group discusses Miami's cultural identityómore its own country than a Florida cityódefined by Spanglish, its own traffic logic, and immigrant food traditions passed down for generations. Clinton finally admits that despite a full week at the W, none of them ever made it into the pool.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The South Beach week closes with a live audience tapingóthe most unhinged episode of the tripóas Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly perform in front of a crowd of fans. Michael's voice is noticeably shot from a long week of celebrating, so Clinton and Carla carry extra energy while he freestyle hosts from notes he promptly loses within 30 seconds. Carla makes her refreshing Pineapple Lime Agua Fresca: blended fresh pineapple with lime water (lime juice diluted to avoid sharpness), ginger, honey, and wateróa batched mocktail or cocktail mixer that's as beautiful as it is easy. She shares her tip for getting full use of citrus by zesting before juicing. The episode features 'Chew It or Screw It,' a voting game where the audience weighs in on Florida's most adventurous foods: iguana pizza, python sausage, alligator ribs, conch fritters, and fried frog legs. Carla and Clinton report on their spontaneous 7 AM sunrise bike rideótheir first ever togetheróand Clinton jokes about his impossibly pale, non-tanning legs. The trio explores Florida's wildlife peculiarities, from iguanas falling from trees in cold snaps to flying cockroaches given affectionate nicknames. A Chewed Up Challenge heads-up guessing game rounds out the audience interaction with giveaways. Season 3's Miami week ends exactly how it began: loud, funny, and hungry.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Season 3 returns to the studio with a spring episode that puts handmade pasta front and center. Michael Symon makes his grandmother's Cavatellióa ricotta-based pasta dough (1 cup flour, 1 egg, 1.5 cups ricotta, Parmesan) that is more forgiving than traditional egg pasta, closer in spirit to a dumpling than a proper noodle. He demonstrates the technique his grandmother taught him as a child: roll the dough into thin strands, cut into inch-long pieces, and press-roll each one with a butter knife to create a hollow, ridged shape that opens slightly when cooked and catches every drop of sauce. Carla has a revelation mid-episode realizing cavatelli is essentially a dumplingóthe Italian version of a global tradition that spans every culture. The sauce is spring itself: brown butter carried almost to the edge, blanched peas, bias-cut asparagus, pasta water, and lemon. Michael also reflects on the memory of cooking as familyópasta making as a group project, a Sunday activity with kids or grandkids, something imperfect that matters anyway. The episode weaves in conversations about spring weather chaos on the East Coast, Gen X collectively aging past the Golden Girls cast, Michael's habit of texting Cleveland friends his Venice Beach weather reports, and a team-meeting story where Nick's extended hand produced entirely different reactions from all three hosts based on their respective cultural backgrounds. Nick debuts a new segment, 'Choose Wisely,' a spring-edition 'most likely to' game. Sponsored by Fresh Direct and Front Gate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Top Chef alum and Carla Hall's culinary producer Ariane Duarte joins the Chewed Up crew to make spring crostini three ways on homemade sourdough focaccia, while the hosts debate food rules and play Chew It or Screw It.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall makes a vibrant frozen pea soup in under 20 minutes—sautéed onions, stock, mint, parsley, white pepper, and cream blended silky smooth—then leads the hosts in Peep Roulette, a blind flavor-guessing game. Clinton wins; Michael is traumatized.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this “Munch Madness” episode of Chewed Up, Carla Hall, Clinton Kelly, and Michael Symon put eight frozen pizzas through a bracket-style taste test inspired by March Madness. The hosts compare each pairing, breaking down crust, sauce, cheese, and overall flavor while mixing in humor, nostalgia, and brutally honest reactions. As they move through the rounds, the conversation is fueled by strong opinions, unexpected preferences, and plenty of debate about what actually makes a good pizza. Michael’s love of authentic pizza drives passionate critiques, Clinton reflects on his long hiatus from frozen options, and Carla keeps things grounded in everyday practicality for busy households. The competition builds toward a final showdown that sparks lively discussion and a few surprising takes. The episode then shifts into a conversation about potlucks, outdoor dining, and picnics, blending personal preferences with playful arguments. It wraps with a fan giveaway and the hosts’ signature banter.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly welcomes Stacy London for a fun, nostalgic episode focused on creative ways to use leftover hard-boiled eggs. Stacy makes her signature deviled eggs with a creamy filling and bold toppings like chorizo and jalapeño, while Clinton shares a simple asparagus mimosa finished with grated eggs and vinaigrette. Along the way, the hosts swap stories, talk fashion, and play games, bringing humor and energy to an episode that blends easy recipes with playful conversation.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Hometown Week brings Michael Symon's roots to the Chewed Up kitchen, as he makes his grandfather Pap's potato and farmer's cheese pierogis—a dish that traces back to the heavy Eastern European influence in both Cleveland, where Michael was born, and Pittsburgh, where his parents grew up. The dough is Pap's recipe: all-purpose flour, a pinch of salt, one egg, sour cream for tenderness, and softened butter worked in for extra fat and pliability—a forgiving dough that rests in the fridge for an hour before being rolled and cut into rounds. The filling is pure simplicity: Idaho potato riced and folded with farmer's cheese in a 2:1 ratio, seasoned with chives, salt, and pepper. Michael boils the pierogis until they float, then finishes them in a pan with caramelized onions, butter, a splash of pasta water, and fresh chives—garnished tableside with sour cream. The episode's centerpiece story is the birth of Lola's famous beef cheek pierogi: when it first went on the Cleveland menu in 1997, diners balked at 'beef cheek,' and Michael sold only one or two a night. He renamed it 'pot roast pierogi,' sold 70 the next night, ran it for a year until diners were hooked, then quietly switched the name back to beef cheek. It became the restaurant's most popular appetizer by a factor of ten. Carla mentions that her nephew AJ is addicted to Michael's GoldBelly pierogis—available in potato and cheese, pork and sauerkraut, and beef cheek. The episode features three debate topics: leftovers vs. the original meal (unanimous chew it, with the exception of reheated steak and fish); the simplest dishes being the hardest to execute (cacio e pepe, French omelet, pie crust, biscuits, vanilla ice cream); and breakfast for dinner (universal love, best kept occasional—or as a late-night eggs tradition after a couple of old fashioneds). The game is Michael's Two Truths and a Lie: he slipped on butter and dropped a full tray at Lola (true); he set off a fire alarm while cooking at a friend's house (true); and he once cooked pierogis on a George Foreman Grill and melted the plastic drip tray (the lie—Clinton guesses correctly, consistent with Michael's famous contempt for George Foremans, air fryers, and microwaves). Fan mail covers: favorite cake (Clinton: carrot cake with raisins; Michael: coconut; Carla: German chocolate); what to do with canned crab (Michael: fold it into a pierogi filling or make angry crab pasta; Carla: red sauce pasta with crab); and why chefs hate tilapia (muddy flavor, overcrowded farming, general bad juju). The fan giveaway offers a monogrammed Michael Symon apron from his Frontgate collection and a copy of Simply Symon Suppers—won by Sue from Columbus, Georgia. Sponsored by FreshDirect and Front Gate.

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino

Plus he shows the viewers a series of simple techniques to create elegant topping to dress it all up. Simple clear instructions with step by step demonstrations on how to get it done in one try!

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino
programme.desc: Chef Stellino reconnects with his Italian roots as he guides the viewer through some simple and exciting techniques on how to make traditional Italian pasta dishes with the elegance and panache of an Italian born chef, with a plateful of tips, techniques and ideas to help anyone improve their style and performance in the kitchen.

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino
programme.desc: Easy does it!, a delicious way to prepare a simple dish packed with flavor and panache, with easy to follow step by step instructions and plating directions. An old school recipe made simple for today's busy home chefs.

programme.title: Cooking with Nick Stellino
programme.desc: Nick prepares French toast with butter braised strawberries in a cassis sugar, matzo brei, a breakfast burrito and kir royale.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques suggests that mastering simple skills and techniques will make your life easier and when you’re comfortable in the kitchen, you’ll be more creative and avoid the trap of a quick fix with a frozen meal…or worse! He teaches you how to sharpen knives, cut and prepare vegetables and eggs and finally make a delicious gratin.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacues knows that most of us want to eat a healthy diet with lots of organic produce. Now so much is available at farmers markets and in grocery stores, you don’t have to spend a fortune if you chose what’s in season when it tastes the best. But for those times you’re in a hurry, it’s okay to grab prewashed, precut or frozen vegetables to make your life easier.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques shows that with a well-stocked pantry and a few good cooking techniques, you can make a tasty dish or a simple meal. His pantry essentials include potato flakes for a simple soup, spaghetti for a quick main dish, lentils, canned salmon, polenta and finally, in the freezer, a poundcake that can be transformed into a celebration dessert.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Gathering with family and friends around the table or for a game of boules is the life’s greatest pleasure for Jacques. He makes chicken wings, cucumber soup, merguez sausage, zucchini canapes, hummus and his father’s Fromage Fort. Simple food that can be made ahead means that you can enjoy a glass of wine with your company.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques loves seafood and feels lucky to live by Long Island Sound with its wonderful stocks of Atlantic fish and shellfish. He prepares scallops, crab and cod––raw, fried, sauteed or grilled in dishes that are quick, simple and delicious.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: When the weather heats up it’s time for family dishes that work on the grill or in the pan. Jacques' great summer menu with Asparagus in Butter Sauce, a Grilled Steak with Zucchini, topped with Anchovy Butter, Curly Dogs for the kids. He shows you classic ways to cut potatoes and ends with a Patriotic Raspberry and Blueberry Galette.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: Sara dives into Chinese heritage at Chinatowns on either side of the continent. In Vancouver, recipes for appetizers cooked with Chinese tea and Dim Sum made by an opera singing Chinese local. Then – a lesson in making Chinese dumplings. Back at home, Wok Queen Grace Young describes her efforts to preserve New York's Chinatown as she and Sara make a BBQ Pork and Yangchow fried rice.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: Sara follows a trail in the Mississippi Delta that leads straight to where blues is born. She keeps going to some of the richest farmland on earth where space age tractors bring in the harvest. Here they eat what they grow, and she joins the farmers to make an easy rice casserole and Blackened catfish. Later – the mother of all hometown cookbooks – Charleston Receipts

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: Holidays are when Sara pulls out the stops when it comes to sweets and desserts. Try her Glazed Pears and shortbread pecan praline, or the surprising twist on potstickers filled with dried plum and apricots. On Ask Sara, tips on blind baking a pie crust. Then a favorite food gift in Sara's family - homemade Chocolate Peppermint Bark.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: Sara's in Barcelona to sip gin and tonics in the shadow of the iconic Sagreda Familia, then joins a food writer to make classic Iberico Pork with Romanesco sauce and Gambas Ajillo. At home, Sara whips up Fideo - Barcelona's take on Paella made with noodles instead of rice.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The Cornwall Castle, Part 1: Mar gets a phone call from another time period. The new owners of an honest-to-goodness castle want to restore and renovate their new property using Mar's 6 Casual Luxury design principles as a guide. It could be a Margical makeover of this hidden-away treasure, but there is a lot of work to do. Mar meets the enthusiastic family and learns their era-honoring plans.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The Cornwall Castle, Part 2: Mar lets down the drawbridge to show you the big reveal! Mar heads back to the castle to check the progress, and for the grand tour of the final results. His 6 Casual Luxury design principles were the owners' guide, and they definitely work in every time period! Watch this distressed property find it's "happily ever after"! (It's a Wow!)

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: We're celebrating 40! 40 original episodes, that is. So Mar’s looking back at three full seasons of Casual Luxury designs. Our favorite moments and yours. Join Mar and the Team on this very special episode as we look back at many of the places, characters, fun, makeovers and trips that make the series special—plus a rare look at the very beginning and some new reveals, too. Celebrate with us!

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar takes Mary Alice—new to Westport—and he just sold her a new condo. He takes her through his Casual Luxury transformation of her new condo step-by-step. Remove walls? Check! Add rooms? Check! Take some risks? Check! A total renovation that requires many hands—and dancing feet—to get to the finish line. But what will she think?

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mary Alice's new condo has gone through many changes on each floor and in every room. Removing walls and adding space has revealed a Casual Luxury environment. After months of renovations and many design decisions, it's time to finally reveal to Mary Alice her new Wonderland. With so much at stake, it's fingers crossed!

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: This home on Punch Bowl Drive in Westport is spacious in every way—except the kitchen. Stuck in its 1970's profile, it feels cramped and ready for a renovation. Mar redesigns this dated kitchen to meet the needs of a 21st-century family. Every bit of space is meticulously reworked with Casual Luxury for function and entertaining.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: There is no place like home but with a break in the design schedule, there's time for an upgrade. So Mar is finally addressing his primary bedroom (and bathroom) at his home Rosebrook Gardens. With space at a premium, how will the wish list for a wall of jaw-dropping built-in closets and more be possible? It's gonna take more than a Casual Luxury design to create the perfect space.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: After Mar finishes his Primary Bedroom reveal to the team they finally see the new primary bath—the oasis of his dreams, an extraordinary use of the space. It's the perfect complement to his new bedroom and brand new built-in storage. Watch his journey renovating his en suite in Casual Luxury style.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar's real estate business is booming and the MARket is hot! When he lists a charming, renovated 1880's home, the family is already out—and so are their furnishings. So he creates Casual Luxury from room to room as usual, this time with a staging partner to help transform the space. Does he get the offers he expects?

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: After selling a client's home in Westport, CT, Mar is asked to design their new New York City apartment. The catch? It has to be completed in under 24 hours! From move in to unpacking to staging….the clock is ticking and the client will be back soon—does Casual Luxury work in the Big Apple and in a time crunch?

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Paul gets a new client call from people who have just downsized. Their new place is great—except for odd layouts and wasted space. They want Mar's magic touch while they are traveling. Not a small project: a total kitchen and primary bedroom & bath demo and reno in less than a month. Can Mar create the perfect space without the client present? Will they love Mar's Casual Luxury designs?

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar continues his rush reno while clients are away. Primary bedroom and bathroom, plus a kitchen—without the client present. A lot is going right—but will it be what they really expect? Despite Mar's award-winning design eye, when the clients return, how highly will they rate these Casual Luxury results?

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: A year in the making! Mar goes rogue using short amounts of time between projects this season and decides to do a complete Casual Luxury backyard and Garden Studio redesign and renovation at his own home. With Paul banned from being on the property, can he sneak a peek before the big reveal to Mar's guests? (Stay tuned for a last minute surprise!)

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Sometimes Mar has to hide things from viewers, but not this episode! This special edition is a true behind-the-scenes of season 4: Oops we can't believe it happened; Yikes, we're showing you; and New: some scenes that didn't quite make it to air. Exclusive, hand-selected, and fun moments you won't see anywhere else. Mar says 'Keep it casual'—if he can keep a straight face.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar has known the Breslin family a long time now—first as home buyers, then as clients, soon as friends, and now as extended family. Union House has evolved with their needs (and with casual luxury), but one last detail remains: the built—but empty—pool house. It needs to be a jewel in time for the summer—and if it's done Mar will get to have a dog-gone party for puppy Clover!

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Tis the season to be MARry! Join Mar and the team for a wonderful and meaningful celebration at Rosebrook Gardens. Wondrously creative holiday touches are everywhere you look, both inside and out. Join the crowds of people who want to come and see for themselves. Treat yourself to lights, decorations, carolers, and lots of good cheer, all done the Casual Luxury way.

programme.title: The Wonders of Hawaii

programme.title: Yosemite Magic

programme.title: A Summer Sunset Into Water

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Space & Planets
programme.desc: This compilation is about Space & Planets! Includes children's songs "Mr. Golden Sun", “Skidamarink” & "More!

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Underwater
programme.desc: This compilation is about Underwater! Includes children's songs "Baby Shark", "Sailor Went To Sea" and More!

programme.title: Carl's Big Adventure - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Carl is visited by three large, very dirty construction vehicles. But Carl’s Car Wash is not big enough to fit them! Carl has an idea! He can supersize his car wash so the vehicles can get a proper scrub-a-dub-dub. He just needs a few parts. With the help of old friends, Carl sets out on a big adventure to the darkest depths of the ocean, the highest heights in the land, and beyond! Don't miss Carl in his first mini-movie as he pushes himself to the limit in the name of vehicular cleanliness!

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Counting and Numbers
programme.desc: The Super Simple show is a compilation of Super Simple’s most popular songs and series that our fans love! This compilation is all about Numbers and Counting! Includes children's song favorites "10 Little Buses", “10 In the Bed” & "10 Little Dinosaurs" and featuring Carl's Car Wash, The Bumble Nums, Sing Along With Tobee, and More!

programme.title: Where Is Baby? - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Peekaboo! Sing along with this adorable song for kids, “Where Is Baby?” Where is baby? Where is baby? Where is baby? There he is.

programme.title: Finny's First Day Of School - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny is a bit nervous on the first day of school, but Myrtle the Turtle is even more anxious and literally hides her shell! Surely Myrtle will come out of her shell when their teacher, a whale named Mr. Magellan, has them draw and present their favorite things to the class! Nope. Myrtle is even more nervous than ever! Being a good friend, Finny helps Myrtle through her anxiety, and the tiny turtle finally shows her drawing to everyone.

programme.title: Baby Shark - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Baby Shark, Mama Shark, Papa Shark, Grandma Shark, and Grandpa Shark are out for a family swim and Baby Shark tries and tries to catch the little fishies but his skills aren’t yet up to the task. Just when he thinks he finally has them a (very big) friend of the little fishies steps in to save the day!

programme.title: Dharma's Digger - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Oh no! While digging deep at a top secret construction site, Dhama’s digger gets splashed with smelly sludge! Everyone says that Carl's Car Wash is the place to get all cleaned up, so Dharma skedaddles over to see how Carl can help. With a quick mess assessment and a few twists of some knobs, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Dharma’s vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! Dharma rolls out one happy customer, and ready to get back to work on the site that Carl is itching to see!

programme.title: Mr. Golden Sun - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Mister Sun, Sun, Mr. Golden Sun...please shine down on me! It’s the Mr. Sun song from Super Simple Songs!

programme.title: Finny's First Sleepover - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: It’s Finny’s first sleepover and Finny is nervous to sleep at Myrtle the Turtle’s house. Throughout the night, Finny becomes more and more comfortable and after a spooky story, Finny becomes the bravest of everyone at the sleepover! Finny can't wait to do a sleepover again – but this time, at Finny’s house!

programme.title: Enchanted Ice Cream - Super Simple
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Enchanted Ice Cream Sundaes! With a map in hand, Humble, Grumble, and Stumble search the orchard for the secret ingredient … and discover a fairy treehouse leading to an enchanted world! The Bumble Nums travel through the portal…but do they find the ingredient they’re looking for? And can they get it back to the kitchen in time for the Cooking Countdown? Watch and see!

programme.title: Hello Hello! - Super Simple Puppets and more
programme.desc: Sing and dance along with the Super Simple Puppets to “Hello Hello!”! It’s a classic Super Simple action song for kids.

programme.title: Erupting Raspberry Lava Cake - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: The Bumble Nums are making an Erupting Raspberry Lava Cake but the secret ingredient is at the top of Mount Fruity Volcano! Can Humble, Grumble, and Stumble climb the volcano and get what they need before it blows? And can they do it in time for the Cooking Countdown? Watch and see!

programme.title: Let's Go For A Drive! - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Get in on the fun and join Milo in his pretend car for a quick ride. Do you see the ducks crossing the road? How about people walking on the sidewalk? Wait, don't cross on a red light!

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet Cats, Dogs, Birds, Bugs and Woodland Creatures! Learn simple words through narration and repetition. Meet all sorts of creatures from domestic kittens to jungle cats, puppies, big and small birds, butterflies, lady bugs, otters and deer!! See whimsical toys and puppets.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As their communication skills continue to develop, little ones begin to recognize common words and may point to objects or pictures when these items are named. Baby Wordsworth playfully taps into your little one's natural curiosity and introduces 30 words from around the home -- both spoken and in sign language. This engaging program presents little ones with familiar objects in the kitchen, playroom, yard and more -- from the living room window to their favorite teddy bear.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: With the Dolittle, and other Baby programs under their belts, the tots who follow Julie Aigner-Clark's Baby Einstein series will sidle up to Baby Newton expecting a similar exploration of building blocks to brilliance, but this time there's a wrinkle or two. Animation makes its first appearance, and parents steeped in keep-it-simple culture may object to the springy "I Know My Shapes" song, a departure from classical-only video predecessors.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones' curiosity about the world blossoms, you can help sow the seeds of discovery with Baby MacDonald: A Day on the Farm. Featuring live-action images, nursery rhymes and fun-filled puppet shows, this playful, interactive program introduces little ones to the exciting sights and sounds of a real working farm.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Between 12 and 24 months, little ones become increasingly interested in more complex ideas and begin to explore numbers and counting. Numbers Nursery introduces little ones to numerals 1 through 5 in a captivating, interactive way that invites them to join in, clap their hands and count out loud.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Within the first year of babies' lives, their physical development begins to kick into high gear as they explore ways to physically interact with the world around them. BABY'S FIRST MOVES is a joyful celebration of their movement milestones -- including first steps, clapping, jumping, dancing and more.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Isn't the world exciting?! Learn about things found in nature like flowers, grass, trees, and leaves with real-life footage and narration. Sing-along to popular nursery rhymes and learn about common bedtime themes. Teach baby how to say words like eat, milk, and all done verbally and through sign language. Discover words and activities about playtime and friendship.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Enjoy the best of summer, autumn and winter with Baby Einstein! Discover the familiar sights and sounds of the different seasons. Learn names of common objects associated with each through repetition and narration and see engaging images of whimsical toys that celebrate each. Discover the magic of each season from falling leaves, rolling beach balls and even snowmen.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones grow, their innate sense of wonder grows too, creating an occasion for celebration with each new discovery. Baby Galileo Discovering the Sky introduces them to that remarkable canopy above us: the sun and moon, fluffy clouds, shimmering stars, colorful planets and whirling galaxies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: The Sandbox
programme.desc: The Sandbox is where our favorite Baby Einstein characters gather to play, problem-solve and have fun. Each five-minute episode explores an area of curiosity relevant to our 2-4 year old audience. Since the four weathered planks of wood that make up the structure of their sandbox are no obstacles for their imaginations, the entire universe is available to them for each new adventure.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Join our playful puppet host, Bard the Dragon, on a fun-filled adventure using everyday words such as flower, cat, and moon, and beautiful scenery from nature. World Of Words lets you and your little one share the excitement of discovering new words together!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Mayta and Jett are ready to draw something big in the ground, but it takes forever. What will Jett and Mayta do?

programme.desc: Tae-Hun's project is finished for the science festival, but Seo-Yoon's robot goes out of control. What will Jett, Tae-Hun, and Seo-Yoon do?

programme.desc: Kian wants to be a giant, but Aiden knocks over Grand Albert's invention, that makes Aiden become a giant. What will Jett, Donnie, Grand Albert, and Kian do?

programme.desc: A dinosaur skeleton is finished to be put in a museum, but a dinosaur skeleton goes after the tow-truck with a magnet. What will Jett, Donnie, Paul, and Justin do?

programme.desc: Jett, Grand Albert, and Margot go back in time to see a real mammoth, but a mother mammoth thinks they are bad guys, until a mother mammoth gets stuck in the hole in the ice. What will Jett, Grand Albert, Margot, and Erk do?

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jules and his grandmother are making a giant waffle, but Jett accidentally adds too much yeast. Can Donnie stop the disaster?

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Oswald has a big piano concert today, but the large piano is too big for him to play by himself. What will Jett and Oswald do?

programme.title: Super Wings

programme.desc: Ndapana has a package from her friend, Stig from Finland, but she knows it never snows in Namibia, until Donnie builds a giant snow globe, and Astra adds snow inside, but the giant snow globe rolls over. What will Jett, Donnie, Astra, and Ndapana do?

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Ivan lets Luna doggercise, but Luna gets away, after Jerome accidentally throws a ball too far away. How will Jett, Jerome, and Ivan get Luna back?

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Satomi's cat Tora gets taken to the recycling center by mistake. How will Jett, Satomi and her mother get Tora back?

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Nadya and her Super Wings friends are able to dance ballet, but Nadya's father needs help getting back home. Can Nadya and her Super Wings friends save the day?

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Iulia's grandfather makes the mechanical robot dinosaurs come to live, but all except Rex go hay-wired, because of the rain. How will Jett, Noah, and Iulia save the day?

programme.desc: Tails brings a new friend home to Knothole - a baby Terra-Pod who has strayed from the herd that is currently migrating. Sonic, Sally, and some of the other Freedom Fighters return the Baby-T to its mom and the rest of the herd.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic reluctantly takes Tails with him to the Big City to find some cotter pins for the Freedom Fighters catapults. While in the City, Sonic is nearly eaten alive by his old dog Muttski, now a roboti-sized guard dog in the Junk Heaps

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters, with new addition, Dulcy, develop a De-Roboti-sizer and try it out on Bunnie. It's a success and, against Sally's wishes, Sonic battles Robotnik's newest weapon, a Virtual Reality Shriek-Bot.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters run into a Swashbuckler, named Ari, battling Swat-Bots. Sonic diverts them, allowing Ari to escape. After hearing that Robotnik is holding his band of Freedom Fighters hostage, Ari decides to help Sonic free them.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic attempts a solo rescue to prevent prisoners from being roboti-sized and is hit by a Memory Scrambler that erases short-term memory.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs steal historical artifacts of Mobuis from Robotnik before he can pawn them off at an auction.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A group of Freedom Fighters is obliterated by a swarm of metallic fly-bots. When Manic goes looking for the insects, he is stung with serum.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A speed enhancing fluid deemed Sonic Tonic is created by Robotnik to make him as fast as Sonic so that he may finally catch him.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs congregate on the Floating Island in their latest effort to find their mother. But Sleet and Dingo also have an interest in this island.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: As Sonic begins to chow chili dogs for a snack, he finds a message in the sack from his mother urging him to head to Speedster Island.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonic becomes aggravated that maybe their efforts of fighting for freedom seem pointless. Sonia suggests that they go to the Oracle for advice.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Robotnik has his eyes set upon Queen Aleena's crown jewel, but Sonic, Manic and Sonia plan to retrieve the gem before Robotnik.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonic, Manic and Sonia find an abandoned baby in an alley and begin to look for the baby's parents. Manic becomes very fond of the child.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Robotnik announces that he will marry Queen Aleena in a plot to become the king of Mobius.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: First in a four-part Time Travel series. Robotnik invents a time machine to go back in time and gather four powerful gemstones. His first attempt is on the high seas, but Sonic goes into hyper-Sonic-overdrive, and reverses time to go after him. ...

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik goes back to medieval times, and enlists the help of Merlink the Magician to get his gemstone.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: It's another windy winter day, and the postal bees are staying indoors-the wind is too strong for their berry bitty wings. "We don't know when the next mail will get through!" It's bad news for Blueberry, who has been anxiously awaiting the next installment of a Patty Persimmon Detective story. If only she could get to the post office in Berry Bitty Dale where her package is waiting!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry, Cherry and the girls form a new band, Strawberry and the Sweet Beats, and prepare to go on the road to perform at the Opening Ceremony of the Berry Big City Games! While master mechanic Berrykin Bruce turns Cherry's tour bus into the band's hotel-on-wheels, Strawberry and her friends pack for the big trip. But the rest of Berry Bitty City points out that someone needs to run the girls' businesses while they are gone.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Berrykins Ed and Earl report to Princess Berrykin and Strawberry that they are certain there is a monster in the berry patch! They saw something with many noses and many tails making weird noises in the bushes. But upon further investigation by Strawberry and her friends, it is discovered that friendly boy Huckleberry Pie was driving his pet-mobile of orphan dogs through the countryside, took a wrong turn and got stuck near Berry Bitty City.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Blueberry is disappointed in the girls' lack of enthusiasm for the Patti Persimmon mystery novels, her favorite book series. She finds herself missing the company of her fellow Persimmon fan Huckleberry, and thinks up a great way to get him to visit. She invites Huck to co-write a story with her to submit to an online mystery magazine.. Huck eagerly accepts and comes for a visit.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: When Huckleberry and Blueberry's Patti Persimmon-style mystery story is rejected by an online mystery magazine, Blueberry and Huck have to come up with a new way to raise support for Huckleberry's Hotel for Homeless Pets back in Berry Big City. Strawberry suggests they hold a big dog show! Plum makes over-ambitious plans to display talents that her dog does not quite have, trying to teach him complicated dance routines.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry and the girls excitedly plan to put on a "Story Time On Stage" musical play for the Berrykin kids: "Berryella and Prince Berry Charming," directed and choreographed by Plum, with musical direction by Cherry. Strawberry will play Berryella, with Lemon and Blueberry as her comically jealous stepsisters, and costume designer Raspberry as her Fairy Fashionmother.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Blueberry and Huck turn the Baby Berrykins' favorite fairy tale, "The Littlest Berrykin," into a comedy play. They pitch it to director Plum and the assembled cast and crew using a large storybook. As Huck and Blueberry narrate the story, we push in on the book and its illustrations transition into animation. In the story, the King and Queen of the Berry Patch (played by Huck and Blueberry) are retiring to the tropics.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Orange wants to spend the day teaching her puppy to fetch, but the frisky little gal doesn't seem to understand the "retrieve" part of the activity-as soon as she gets the ball, she runs out of sight and returns moments later without it. Orange gets a new ball and tries again, with the same results. Over and over, until Orange is out of balls. Puzzled and frustrated that she can't find where her pup has stashed them, she goes to enlist her friends to help her search.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: With the power completely out in Berry Bitty City, the girls go on a camping trip to be out of the way of the Berrykin work crews. Lemon is nervous because she believes there is a creepy creature in the forest (that she read about in a book Blueberry loaned her), and the girls get the nervous bug, but Strawberry turns their apprehension into fun by daring her friends to stay out in the woods the longest!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: The girls are driven to distraction by their frisky puppies frolicking all over Berry Bitty City! Full of energy and thinking that everything in town is a toy for them to play with, they cause comical mayhem wherever they go. Strawberry suggests the pups need a place of their own where they can play, a dog park! The girls decide to call in Huckleberry, who runs an animal hotel in Berry Big City, to help design the park.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: The girls plan a formal afternoon lawn party tea to inaugurate their new dog park. Plum is in charge of decorations, Cherry music, and Strawberry snacks. There's even a giant, dog-shaped ice sculpture. As the girls get dressed, Raspberry puts the finishing touches on the high-fashion hats that will accompany their beautiful gowns. At last everything is ready. But when the gate opens... the puppies back away!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: To convince the dance-averse Huckleberry to attend the big masquerade ball at the dog park, Blueberry promises she'll stay by his side the whole evening and make sure he doesn't have to dance. But as the day of the ball dawns, Blueberry awakens with a bad cold. She doesn't know how to tell Huck that she can't go to the ball. Lemon and Raspberry misinterpret her sniffles, thinking she's so heartbroken to be disappointing Huck that she's crying!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Lemon and Raspberry hire twins Sweet & Sour to help out in Strawberry's café, so Strawberry will have time to appear in an internet fashion contest.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry's world-traveling cousin Apple Dumplin' stops at Berry Bitty City. What starts as a brief visit becomes a series of comical misadventures that ends with Apple finding a new home with Strawberry and her friends.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Lemon and Raspberry's fashion podcast is saved by Apple, then almost sabotaged by Sweet and Sour when Lemon's "mood hair" runs amok, acting like the bickering twins, and the twins' comical argument is accidentally broadcast on the "berrynet."

programme.title: Rick Steves Art of Europe
programme.desc: In the 1800s, the Industrial Revolution spawned new artistic styles: idealized Romanticism, light-chasing Impressionism, sensuous Art Nouveau. Then Europes tumultuous 20th century inspired rule-breaking art as exciting as the times: from Expressionism and Cubism to Surrealism to Abstract. The genius of artists like Van Gogh, Picasso, and Dal express the complexity of our modern world.

programme.title: Rick Steves Art of Europe
programme.desc: As the Ice Age glaciers melted, European civilization was born and with it, so was art. From the Stone Age came prehistoric art: mysterious tombs, mighty megaliths, and vivid cave paintings. Then the Egyptians and the Greeks laid the foundations of Western art creating a world of magical gods, massive pyramids, sun-splashed temples, and ever-more-lifelike statues.

programme.title: Rick Steves Art of Europe
programme.desc: The Romans gave Europe its first taste of a common culture and awe-inspiring art. From its groundbreaking architecture to its statues, mosaics, and frescos, Rome engineered bigger and better than anyone before. At its peak, the Roman Empire was a society of unprecedented luxury, with colossal arenas for entertaining the masses and giant monuments to egotistical emperors. And then it fell.

programme.title: Rick Steves Art of Europe
programme.desc: After Rome fell, Europe spent a thousand years in its Middle Ages. Its art shows how the light of civilization flickered in monasteries and on Europes fringes: Christian Byzantium, Moorish Spain, and pagan Vikings. Then, around A.D. 1000, Europe rebounded. The High Middle Ages brought majestic castles, radiant Gothic cathedrals, and exquisite art that dazzled the faithful and the secular alike.

programme.title: Rick Steves Art of Europe
programme.desc: Around 1400, Europe rediscovered the aesthetics of ancient Greece and Rome. This rebirth of classical culture showed itself in the statues, paintings, and architecture of Florence, then spread to Spain, Holland, Germany, and beyond. The Renaissancefrom art-loving popes to Leonardos Mona Lisa and Michelangelos David celebrated humanism and revolutionized how we think about our world.

programme.title: They Volunteered for This: Merrill's Marauders
programme.desc: MERRILL'S MARAUDERS: THEY VOLUNTEERED FOR THIS tells the fascinating story of a group of World War II soldiers known as Merrills Marauders who were one of the most heroic and least known combat units to serve in the war. Fighting in Burma in 1944-1945 under the legendary General Frank Merrill, the Marauders were all volunteers who specialized in hit and run tactics in the jungles and mountains of the region. In this documentary, veterans recount the brazen tactics and clever strategies they used to outwit and defeat the enemy. They were not supplied very well and had to use mules to move from place to place to carry what supplies they did have. The Japanese always thought the 3,000 soldiers were a group of 15,000 due to their success of being everywhere at every time, and because of the damage and casualties they were able to inflict. What the Marauders did was miraculous considering the environment in which they fought and the determined enemy they fought against. In 2022 the Marauders received the Congressional Gold Medal in recognition of their sacrifice and accomplishments. Narrated by television news icon Tom Brokaw, the documentary includes vivid recollections of the veterans who volunteered for the mission, enhanced by archival combat footage from the war.

programme.title: The Seabees on Iwo Jima
programme.desc: THE SEABEES ON IWO JIMA focuses on the United States Naval Construction Battalions in World War II, which built all the infrastructure for the Allies in Europe and the Pacific in WWII. The Seabees were construction workers by trade and signed up to become part of a special battalion in the war that built the airfields, runways, and bases needed in the war. The Seabees, some 60 years of age, were best known for their work on coral islands in the Pacific, where they helped build runways out of the jungle which helped the United States take the war right to Japan's doorstep in 1945.The Seabee's motto was We Build; We Fight. Not only did these men have to build bases, but many times they had to put down their shovels or get off their bulldozers and pick up a rifle or machine gun to fend off enemy attacks. The Seabee's most violent fight was on Iwo Jima, where they went ashore in the first wave with the Marines. It's on Iwo Jima where their motto We Build; We Fight was most famously put to the test in World War II.

programme.title: Behind the Wings
programme.desc: A continued exploration of the Artemis missions, building on Episode 404 of Behind the Wings. Look back at Apollo to understand what we learned from going to the moon for the first time. Follow the progress of the program, from the development of new lunar architecture to the training of the astronauts who will take the next steps on the moon. In a Virtual Reality lab at Lockheed Martin Space, Engineers discuss the design of the Orion spacecraft. At Kennedy Space Center, technicians reflect on the learnings from Artemis I. At NASA's Johnson Space Center, astronauts are training for Artemis II, the first Artemis crewed mission. Meet the diverse team working on the program and discover how returning to the Moon is a crucial step towards new scientific discoveries, a cis-lunar economy, and inspiring the next generation of explorers.

programme.desc: Experience the timeless charm of a log pile ablaze with cozy firelight. Natural wood textures and subtle pops bring a cabin-in-the-woods feel to your living room.

programme.desc: Instantly turn your screen into a beautiful fireplace with lifelike flames, glowing embers, and soothing crackles—the perfect background for your holiday celebrations.
